WILL SPEAK FOR SUFFRAGE.
Mrs. J. NJ Reynolds will speak
in the interests of the equal suf
frage amendment now pending in
Washington as follows:
Friday night, Aug. 10. in the
county court house at Colfax.
Saturday evening, in the Congre-
gational church at Five Mile
Prairie.


Sunday evening. in the Union
Park Methodist church, corner of
Third and Nelson. Miss Fay Nor
villius will sing.
Monday evening. Aug. %. in the
Congregational church at Kenne-
wick.


Tuesday evening, Methodist
church at Pasco.
Wednesday evening, North Yak
ina.


Thursday evening, Ellensburg.

L. o. T. M. WILL MEET.
The Ladies of the Maccabees of
the World will hold their regular
hive review Saturday evening,
August 2, at s o'clock at upper Pa
cific halls.
| z 4
COLLEGE PICNIC.
| The alumni, friends and former
students of Whitman college will
enjoy days outing Thursday.
August 25, at Liberty lake.
| 4 4 4


W. c. T. v. ARE GUESTS.


Mrs. Seaman entertained the
members of the Central w. c. T. U.
yesterday afternoon at her home,
1894 Spokane street.
| A feature of the program was
the paper on Lucy Webb Hayes, $
read by Mrs. Cram. The catachism
was given by Mrs. Dewesse.


4 4 4


LADIES' AID MET.


The members of the Ladies' aid
of the Lidgerwood Congregational
church met yesterday afternoon
with Mrs. Otis Davenport at her
home on Bridgeport avenue.


Z =


WHIST PARTY.
The Women Of WOodcraft, Prim.
rose circle. will give social whist
party tonight at lower Pacific halls


& &
WILL VISIT At NEWMAN LAKE


Miss Edith Morrison will leave
Monday for Newman lake. She
will spend two weeks at the Young
Woman's Christian association cot
tage.
